name: Moche Stirrup Spout Vessel
primary_sources: University of California, Los Angeles photographic collection

visual_attributes.morphology: stirrup spout vessel with a human face effigy
visual_attributes.texture: smooth ceramic surface with painted slip
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: ranges from 6–45 cm in height, typically 15–30 cm
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: three-dimensional human face with a stirrup spout on top

domain_specific_visual.headdress: decorated with a two-headed bird and feathers

domain_specific_functional.cultural_role: depicts Moche rulers or elite members
domain_specific_functional.use_context: used during life and placed in graves

relationships.description: Created by the Moche culture of Peru
Similar headdresses found in the tomb of the warrior priest god

contextual_attributes.origin: Moche culture, north coast of Peru
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Moche Valley, Virú Valley, north coast of Peru
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents Moche rulers and elite, used in ceremonial and funerary contexts
primary_sources: archaeological discoveries in the North Coast
